questions.question: How many chapters (Surahs) are there in the Holy Quran?
questions.explanation: The Holy Quran contains 114 chapters (Surahs) of varying lengths.

questions.question: What is the first revelation that came to Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)?
questions.explanation: The first revelation was the beginning of Surah Al-Alaq, starting with 'Iqra' (Read).

questions.question: How many times should Muslims pray daily?
questions.explanation: Muslims are required to perform Salah (prayer) five times daily: Fajr, Dhuhr, Asr, Maghrib, and Isha.

questions.question: What is the Islamic pilgrimage to Mecca called?
questions.explanation: Hajj is the annual Islamic pilgrimage to Mecca, one of the Five Pillars of Islam.

questions.question: In which month do Muslims observe fasting?
questions.explanation: Muslims observe fasting during the month of Ramadan, the ninth month of the Islamic calendar.

questions.question: What is Zakat?
questions.explanation: Zakat is the obligatory charity that Muslims must give to help the poor and needy.

questions.question: Who was the first Caliph after Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)?
questions.explanation: Abu Bakr was the first Caliph (successor) after Prophet Muhammad (PBUH).

questions.question: What is the Islamic declaration of faith called?
questions.explanation: Shahada is the Islamic declaration of faith: 'There is no god but Allah, and Muhammad is His messenger.'

questions.question: Which city is considered the holiest in Islam?
questions.explanation: Mecca (Makkah) is the holiest city in Islam, birthplace of Prophet Muhammad and location of the Kaaba.

questions.question: What is the Islamic New Year called?
questions.explanation: The Islamic New Year begins with the month of Muharram, the first month of the Islamic calendar.
